opts: Stay with your assignment and trust a teammate will pick up the trailing attacker fast
Pick up the late attacker — your winger on that side needs to find their assignment
Yell switch to your winger and hold your current man until the coverage sorts itself
Skate toward the trailer and leave your D partner alone to handle the two attackers
Yes — pinch down low and help your winger win the battle before the defender closes on him
No — your winger has possession and your half-wall winger can support. Hold the blue line.
Yes — any time a teammate is battling in the corner you should crash down from the point
Skate to the net-front and look for a rebound while your winger fights for the puck alone
Shoot the puck right now before the forwards close the lane — any shot on net during a power play creates a chance
Fake the shot to freeze them, then pass to the half-wall player who opened up when they committed to you
Hold the puck and try to skate around the two closing forwards to open a fresh shooting lane for yourself
Pass it back to your other point defender and let them try a shot from up high once the forwards turn around
Both skate straight at the D so the double pressure forces a quick turnover behind the net before a pass goes out
You force the D's direction from the right, your linemate cuts off the outlet to the left — trap them between you
You back off to the high slot and let your linemate handle the forecheck on their own down below the dots
Both pull back to the blue line and set up the neutral zone trap so the other team has to dump the puck in
Chase the puck deep into the corner and try to strip the puck before they pass
Hold your position and intercept the first outlet pass — you're the plug
Skate back to your defensive zone right away so you're ready to cover the slot
Pressure the puck carrier with full speed and force a rushed pass up the wall
Wait until all three of your forwards line up across the neutral zone together
Go immediately — the 3-on-1 window closes fast as the other team gets back
Regroup with your D at your own blue line to set up a slower controlled attack
Dump the puck in deep and forecheck hard to try to recover it in their zone
Slow down and wait for your team to get on side — attacking two pinching D is risky here
Skate hard behind them — you have a breakaway if you beat them to their blue line
Pass across to a winger on the far side — switch the play to the weak side for a clean rush
Dump the puck deep past both D and chase hard because they're pinching too high to recover
Force a crisp pass to a covered teammate — a sharp pass can break coverage if the defender doesn't react
Wheel to the other side of the net — moving forces the defender to move and may open a teammate
Power through the defender with your lower body and drive the puck out in front for a shot on goal
Go down to the ice and look at the ref so a hooking call gets made on the defender leaning on you here
Attempt the long stretch pass up to the blue line and hope it sneaks past the two closing defenders
Protect the puck tight to the boards with your body, wait for the angle to open, then make a short chip pass
Spin off the boards through both defenders with quick footwork and create your own lane out of the corner
Rim the puck hard around the boards to the other side and trust that a teammate is breaking there to pick it up
Give up the chase and skate back calmly so you're ready for the next rush after the scoring chance is done
Get your stick into their lane — angle them toward the boards and get your body in front of the shooting lane
Reach out with your free hand to grab or hook their jersey before they get to a dangerous shooting area
Dive forward and slide on your belly to block the shot — a body on the ice can take away the whole shooting lane
Keep moving with the fake and commit hard so the attacker can't beat you back again
Stop your movement, reset your feet, and regain your gap — don't fully commit
Drop to the ice and slide to block the shot before a clean release gets off
Poke check right now so the attacker can't come back to the forehand side
Hold the puck at the half-wall and wait for a cleaner passing option to open up on the power play
Thread the seam pass to the middle player — the PK forward cheating toward you opened that
Shoot from the half-wall — any shot on net with a power-play advantage is a good one to take
Pass the puck back to the point and let your defender try to shoot it through from up high
Go for the shorthanded goal — attack the net with speed on the 2-on-3
Clear the zone and get back — a clean clear is a win on the PK
Pass it back to your goalie so they can freeze the puck for a whistle
Dump it back into your own zone and let the other team come get it
Standing still with your stick flat on the ice so the passer has a wide easy target to aim for
Looking up and deciding where you're going with the puck before it arrives — pre-reading the play
Calling loudly for the puck so the passer hears your voice and knows exactly where to aim it
Watching the puck all the way onto your blade so you don't fumble the reception on a hard flat pass
Keep cycling — pass back down to the corner and try again on the next rotation
Shoot immediately into the open slot or pass to a teammate breaking there
Hold the puck at the half-wall and wait for more teammates to support you
Drop the puck back to the point defenseman for a shot through the open lane
Pass down to the corner — find a teammate low and start a cycle below the goal line
Shoot from the half-wall — you have time and the slot defender is distracted
Pass up to the point defender for a one-timer — the slot defender isn't watching you
Drop the puck behind you for a trailing teammate to pick up since nobody is pressuring
Force a hard pass to a covered teammate — a crisp pass can break coverage if it hits the tape before the forechecker reacts
Wheel and carry it out — both forecheckers are committed to your forwards. The D carrying it out is the best option.
Shoot the puck around the boards — rim it hard along the wall past the forecheckers and let your winger chase it up the ice
Wait behind the net with the puck — hold it there until the forecheckers commit and then decide based on what they do next
Skate full speed right at the defender and try to blow by them wide outside
Skate with control, read their skates, and make a move when they commit
Shoot from the far blue line before the defender can close off the lane on you
Slow down and wait for your teammates to catch up before attacking the zone
Stay with the attacker you started with — never switch assignments in your own zone ever
Take the attacker coming to your side, let your partner clean up their original man
Skate to the front of the crease and stay tight to your goalie as an extra body in front
Back up toward the crease and let the attacker cross while your D partner recovers behind
Chase the puck carrier into the corner at top speed — full pressure can force a bad pass out
Read their breakout pass options and cut them off — be in the lane before the pass is
Back off to the blue line and reset — let them break out clean and set up neutral zone defense
Change lines right away since you're tired — let fresh legs come on for the next forecheck
Attack the net right away — try to score another goal so the lead becomes safer with 90 seconds left
Control the puck along the boards, protect it, and force the other team to come get it — use every second
Dump the puck deep into the other team's zone and forecheck hard to keep the puck pinned into their corner
Pass the puck around your own zone and hold possession until the final horn sounds on the clock
High to the blocker side — beat them over the shoulder
Low and to one side — an aggressive goalie is exposed low
Straight at the chest and hope for a rebound chance
Deke wide around the goalie since they are out of position
Pass the puck around your zone until a perfect breakout option opens up
Get the puck out of your zone as fast as possible — any clean clear is a win
Hold the puck tight in your corner to kill as much time off the penalty as possible
Try to skate up and score a shorthanded goal on the rush before they set up
Chase both attackers hard and try to score on the rush coming the other way
One forward pressures the puck, the others recover their positions — don't panic
All five players retreat to the front of your net and pack the slot tight for defense
Ignore the rush and stay up ready for the next offensive chance when your team recovers
Leave your spot every time the puck moves so you can keep steady pressure on whoever has it
When a player with the puck is in a dangerous spot — near the slot or about to pass to the slot
Never leave your position — stay planted at your spot no matter where the puck moves on the ice
Only leave when your D partner calls for help on the puck below the goal line in the corner
Skate to the bench and get off for a quick line change
Pressure the puck carrier immediately to slow the breakout
Skate back toward your own blue line to set up defensively
Stand still in the neutral zone and watch the play develop
Ignore the D and pass down to the corner — keep the cycle going low
Pass to the D who stepped in — they now have a shot from a dangerous area
Shoot from the corner — a bad-angle shot can still create a juicy rebound chance
Pass up the boards to a winger — reset the play because the point is wide open now
Force the pass to the covered strong-side winger — the puck will sort itself out
Pass to the open left winger on the short side — shortest distance, cleanest
Carry the puck out yourself through the middle of the neutral zone with speed
Rim the puck hard around the boards to the far side and hope for a clean chase
Chase after the point man and pressure the shot from outside the zone
Push the screener out of the crease area — your goalie needs to see
Stay in the passing lane between their two point defenders and block it
Back up toward your own goal line and protect the front of the net
